overall.rationale: Applicable gate failures and surviving blockers remain in every unit; Nyxa fails the deliberate-horror exception, and the group drifts from the approved hard-matte Vellum finish.

group_convergence_findings.evidence: Cashmere, Kett, and Sable converge on dark fitted leather, boots, belts, and soot-black tactical tailoring in P004/P006.
Cashmere, Quillith, Luna, and Sable vary ethnicity but retain closely similar lean fashion-model proportions and stern beauty treatment.
Korath, Cashmere, Quillith, Kett, Luna, and Sable all default to brow compression or stern scowls instead of their recorded contradictions in P003.
Cashmere, Quillith, and Luna repeat fitted waists, long split drapes, boots/bare-leg display, and similar upright fashion posing.
Ledger, siphon hand, bows, blade ring, branding iron, correction plane, fist, ligament, clamp, and rifle remain materially varied.
The nonhuman silhouettes are distinct and do not converge on repeated horns, wings, or one demon body plan.
Trait reads are embodied in bodies, garments, or dominant props; no unit requires floating VFX.
P006 shows the Phase 2 masters as dark photoreal/CG character renders with wet highlights and polished leather/metal, unlike Vellum's dry illustrative surface separation.

blockers.effect: Professional style gate fails across the cut.
blockers.closure_condition: Fresh equal-scale comparison shows dry illustrative cloth, skin, paper, bone, and metal with no wet/plastic or polished-leather drift on every master.

blockers.effect: Roster psychology converges into generic sternness.
blockers.closure_condition: Same-master face and pose recaptures visibly separate each recorded feeling, want, concealed fear, strategy, contradiction, and villainous distortion without repeated scowls.

blockers.effect: Garment individuality is weakened.
blockers.closure_condition: Cashmere, Quillith, Luna, Kett, and Sable no longer share the dark fitted leather/split-drape fashion aisle while preserving role and trait channels.

blockers.effect: Blessed and silhouette-correspondence gates fail.
blockers.closure_condition: The master visibly matches selected A's shoulder-fused burden, reads disgraced before monumental hero, and shows shame plus compulsory miracle cost.

blockers.effect: Face psychology and villain gate fail.
blockers.closure_condition: Face and demanding hand unmistakably show exhausted shame, accusatory debt, and coercive martyrdom rather than a generic grim protector.

blockers.effect: Villain, threat, nonhuman psychology, and horror-exception gates fail.
blockers.closure_condition: Asymmetric closing plates and sensory slits visibly target and imprison; the read cannot be neutral egg, armadillo, or passive shield at full size or 96 px.

blockers.effect: Three-option and selected-master correspondence gate fails.
blockers.closure_condition: Three materially different outer contours are shown and selected B's centered legal-shield ledger/pose is preserved exactly in the master.

blockers.effect: Recorded face psychology is not visible.
blockers.closure_condition: One eye appraises while the other visibly loses focus, with proprietary invitation over concealed panic rather than a generic executive scowl.

blockers.effect: Face psychology reads as generic sly thief.
blockers.closure_condition: The face visibly combines a plea for acceptance with reward-scanning vigilance and imminent theft; body remains adult and predatory.

blockers.effect: Deliberate-horror exception and peak-age contract fail.
blockers.closure_condition: Batlike/nonhuman temporal predation is the first read before elderly woman or crone at master and 96 px scales, with no reliance on the bow alone.

blockers.effect: Face does not carry the recorded contradiction.
blockers.closure_condition: Asymmetric delight and temporal pain visibly coexist, with feral target fixation and ancient grief instead of one sly scowl.

blockers.effect: Visible supernatural cost is ambiguous and finish is slick.
blockers.closure_condition: Erased contours/identity loss read as bodily consequence rather than decorative scars, and gray skin/blades meet the Vellum dry-matte benchmark.

blockers.effect: Threat, face psychology, and visible cost gates fail.
blockers.closure_condition: Paired eyelid tally scars and impaired measurement are legible; smile reads courteous collection predation, and the attack-weighted branding iron remains threatening at 96 px.

blockers.effect: Face psychology and villain gate fail.
blockers.closure_condition: Viewer is pinned as a favored pupil; approval, fear of being surpassed, and controlling correction coexist instead of generic disdain.

blockers.effect: Supernatural cost is decorative rather than causal.
blockers.closure_condition: Burned exposed nerves and leaking annotation are physically readable as the cost of forced recasts, distinct from ornamental crown pins and tattoos.

blockers.effect: Cartel channel and individuality contract fail.
blockers.closure_condition: Remove ornamental chain/coin gangster coding and polished black leather; Cartel reads through stamped ownership scars while the single three-stage fist remains dominant.

blockers.effect: Face and villain psychology collapse to generic bruiser.
blockers.closure_condition: A specific grievance, rigid restraint, and anticipatory relief at impact are visible without generic armored-enforcer anger.

blockers.effect: Villain, threat, and face-psychology gates fail.
blockers.closure_condition: Warm reassurance and cool surveillance visibly split the face and pose; she reads as coercive triangulator, not neutral ribbon dancer or heroic mage.

blockers.effect: Face/pose agreement and villain psychology fail.
blockers.closure_condition: Central face visibly demands maintenance with panicked entitlement; subordinate faces remain victims, and the whole pose supports active predation rather than trapped sadness.

blockers.effect: Dominant clamp anatomy is not legible as an active pinning hand.
blockers.closure_condition: One traceable anatomical clamp visibly opens toward prey and survives at 96 px without reading as a passive bracket or ladder.

blockers.effect: Androgynous lane and roster-distinct gates fail.
blockers.closure_condition: The same nonbinary identity is preserved while body/face styling visibly reads androgynous and separates from the female mage fashion template.

blockers.effect: Face and villain gates fail.
blockers.closure_condition: Moral satisfaction and a suppressed flicker of doubt are visible around the cited/scarred eye; the figure reads self-righteous villain rather than scowling soldier.

rejected_findings.finding: Female non-frontliners fail for lacking armor.
rejected_findings.reason: Armor is not required for Cashmere, Quillith, Luna, Nyxa, or Sable; their roles/protection records make it inapplicable.
Sable is explicitly nonbinary/androgynous; those conditional gates are not applicable.

rejected_findings.finding: Creep fails because it has no human mouth or brows.
rejected_findings.reason: The criteria permit a deliberate nonhuman facial mechanism; hollow apertures, head cant, and erased face can carry psychology.

rejected_findings.finding: Veyra fails solely because its face is hidden.
rejected_findings.reason: A nonhuman slit/seam mechanism is allowed; the failure is its neutral, non-targeting execution.

rejected_findings.finding: Vellum requires copying its costume and floating paper.
All 96 px checks fail because facial details disappear.
rejected_findings.reason: Most dominant silhouettes and props remain recognizable; 96 px is judged on identity and role, not facial microdetail.

protected_dissent.position: A reasonable reviewer may pass its hollow-eye fixation and loaded crouch; Seat A protects that pass while retaining only cost/finish repair.
Disgrace and bodily cost are partially present; dissent may call the Blessed gate repairable rather than redesign-level.
The weapon, ears, split-aging body, and crouch support predation, but Seat A retains the blocker because elderly humanoid reads before batlike anatomy.
Gender identity is not inferred from anatomy; the finding addresses the explicit visual-lane contract and roster differentiation, not Sable's identity.
